name: One Vision
description_short: One Vision Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving Clark, Floyd, and Harrison Counties in Indiana with checking, savings, loans, and credit card products.
description_long:
One Vision Credit Union operates as a community-focused financial institution headquartered in Indiana, serving members in Clark, Floyd, and Harrison Counties. The credit union emphasizes accessibility and member benefits through its cooperative structure, where members are owners rather than customers of a traditional bank.

The institution offers a comprehensive range of financial products including auto loans, mortgages, personal loans (branded as "whatever, whenever loans"), savings and checking accounts, and a Cashback Visa card with 1% cash back on all purchases. Members have access to a nationwide network of surcharge-free ATMs and multiple physical branch locations. The credit union maintains a routing number (283077669) and provides traditional banking services alongside digital access.

One Vision distinguishes itself through its membership-based cooperative model and emphasis on community involvement, highlighted by their "Community Matters" initiatives and an in-house Community Event room. The institution actively engages members through news and event communications and appears to prioritize accessibility, as evidenced by their willingness to assist members with mobility issues during facility renovations. Their marketing emphasizes straightforward, member-friendly language and transparent communication about security protocols.

As a credit union, One Vision operates under member governance and regulatory oversight distinct from commercial banks. However, the publicly available website content provides limited detail regarding specific interest rates, fees, loan terms, or comparative competitive advantages. The institution's service area is geographically limited to three Indiana counties, which restricts membership eligibility. More detailed financial terms and conditions would be necessary for comprehensive evaluation.
services: Cashback Visa credit card with 1% rewards
Surcharge-free ATM access across the nation
Physical branch locations and drive-thru banking
Member services and customer support via phone
pros: 1% cash back on all purchases with Cashback Visa card
Access to surcharge-free ATM network across the nation
Membership eligibility for residents, employees, or students in Clark, Floyd, or Harrison Counties in Indiana
Multiple loan products including auto loans, mortgages, and personal loans
Member-owned cooperative structure with community involvement focus
Proactive security communication and fraud prevention alerts
Physical branch locations with drive-thru and temporary lobby assistance for accessibility
cons: Membership limited to three Indiana counties (Clark, Floyd, Harrison) — not available nationwide
Limited publicly available information on interest rates, APRs, and specific loan terms on website
Recent drive-thru closure at Clarksville location for renovation may create temporary service disruptions
No mention of online account opening or digital membership application process on website
Geographic limitation reduces competitive comparison options for local members
best_for: Residents or employees in Clark, Floyd, or Harrison Counties in Indiana seeking full-service banking
Auto buyers looking for competitive auto loans from a local credit union
Homebuyers in the service area interested in mortgage products
Community-minded individuals who value credit union membership and local involvement
diagnosis: One Vision Credit Union is best for Indiana residents or employees in Clark, Floyd, or Harrison Counties seeking traditional full-service banking from a member-owned cooperative with community focus. The primary caveat is strict geographic membership limitation and lack of detailed public rate/term information online.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
